description: Verify backups include critical runtime data and restore validates archives, requires confirmation, replaces atomically, and retains rollback data.

command: set -euo pipefail; tmp=$(mktemp -d); trap 'rm -rf "$tmp"' EXIT; cp -a agent-system "$tmp/agent-system"; export DIGITAL_OFFICE_SYSTEM_HOME="$tmp/agent-system"; office="$tmp/agent-system/bin/office-system"; mkdir -p "$tmp/agent-system/settings" "$tmp/agent-system/runs/r1" "$tmp/agent-system/logs"; printf '{"mode":"original"}' > "$tmp/agent-system/settings/user-preferences.json"; printf '{"kind":"digital-office-loop-run","run_id":"r1"}' > "$tmp/agent-system/runs/r1/run.json"; printf '{"event":"original"}\n' > "$tmp/agent-system/logs/audit-events.jsonl"; "$office" backup --output "$tmp/backup.tar.gz" >/dev/null; printf '{"mode":"changed"}' > "$tmp/agent-system/settings/user-preferences.json"; set +e; "$office" restore "$tmp/backup.tar.gz" >/dev/null 2>&1; code=$?; set -e; test "$code" -ne 0; "$office" restore "$tmp/backup.tar.gz" --dry-run > "$tmp/dry-run.json"; "$office" restore "$tmp/backup.tar.gz" --confirmed > "$tmp/restored.json"; python3 -c 'import json,sys,pathlib; dry=json.load(open(sys.argv[1])); restored=json.load(open(sys.argv[2])); prefs=json.load(open(sys.argv[3])); assert dry["status"]=="valid"; assert {"settings","runs","logs"} <= set(dry["components"]); assert restored["status"]=="restored" and pathlib.Path(restored["rollback_path"]).exists(); assert prefs["mode"]=="original"' "$tmp/dry-run.json" "$tmp/restored.json" "$tmp/agent-system/settings/user-preferences.json"; python3 -c 'import io,tarfile,sys; t=tarfile.open(sys.argv[1],"w:gz"); i=tarfile.TarInfo("settings/link"); i.type=tarfile.SYMTYPE; i.linkname="../../outside"; t.addfile(i); t.close()' "$tmp/malicious.tar.gz"; set +e; "$office" restore "$tmp/malicious.tar.gz" --dry-run >/dev/null 2>&1; code=$?; set -e; test "$code" -ne 0; python3 -c 'import io,tarfile,sys; t=tarfile.open(sys.argv[1],"w:gz"); i=tarfile.TarInfo("settings/large.txt"); data=b"ab"; i.size=len(data); t.addfile(i,io.BytesIO(data)); t.close()' "$tmp/oversized.tar.gz"; set +e; "$office" restore "$tmp/oversized.tar.gz" --dry-run --max-uncompressed-bytes 1 >/dev/null 2>&1; code=$?; set -e; test "$code" -ne 0
